data.title: Michelin shares climb after better than expected FY results
data.description:
** Shares of Michelin are up 5.3% after the publication of their FY results on Wednesday

** The French tyre maker reported a 5.1% decline in its full-year sales volumes, slightly beating analysts consensus of -5.2%

** Its FY operating income came ahead of consensus at EUR 3.38 billion ($3.52 billion) versus the EUR 3.35 billion expected

** J.P. Morgan says the company delivered a "solid set" of FY results

** The broker points that FCF before acquisitions of EUR 2.2 billion came in strong supporting dividend of EUR 1.38 per share

** "We believe the firm is doing structural work which is currently difficult to see in the absence of volume growth but will become evident once volumes strengthen across the three sub-divisions," JPM says

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 2.1%​ YTD

($1 = 0.9596 euros)

data.title: Kemper Completes Redemption of $450 Million in Senior Notes
data.description: Kemper said late Wednesday that it completed the redemption of $450 million in 4.350% Senior Notes due 2025, using available cash.

data.title: Metso's shares rise on strong order growth, cash flow improvement
data.description:
** Shares in Metso rise 5.7% after the Finnish mining equipment maker reported a better than expected order intake and improved cash flow in Q4

** "The Group's orders received grew by 13 percent, thanks especially to the strong order intake for the Minerals equipment business," CEO Sami Takaluoma said in the statement

** Q4 orders of EUR 1.39 billion ($1.45 billion) were higher than the EUR 1.32 billion expected by analysts on average

** Q4 sales of EUR 1.27 billion also slightly beat estimates

** "Cash flow, which has been a key focus for investors, did improve, aided by a working capital release of €43m, marking a step up from the €4m achieved in 3Q24," J.P.Morgan says

** The shares are on the track for their best day in a year if the gains hold

($1 = 0.9601 euros)

data.title: 'Another weak print, no easy fixes' - Neste slumps on Q4 miss
data.description:
** Shares in Neste slump 12% after it posted core profit below expectations, hit by a decline in renewable fuel sales prices, operational challenges in its renewable refineries

** The Finnish oil refiner and biofuel maker reports Q4 EBITDA of EUR 168 million ($175.12 million), missing the 308 million euros seen by analysts in a company-provided poll

** "Another weak print, no easy fixes," RBC says, pointing to collapsed Renewable Products premiums

** Jefferies notes Q4 EBITDA missed consensus driven by Renewable Products unit, while Neste's outlook for 2025 is cautious

** Jefferies says that dividend cut to 0.2 euros per share from 1.2 euros "is higher than expected but should alleviate balance sheet concerns and is sustainable"

** "We wonder whether this will be enough to bring investors back to the name given the continued macro challenges," RBC adds

** Neste, heading for its worst day in 9 months, sits among worst performers on Europe's benchmark STOXX 600 index

($1 = 0.9593 euros)

data.title: Unilever CEO: Ice Cream's Primary Listing Will Be In Amsterdam
Nissan Maintains FY Global Sales Forecast at 3.4M Units
India's Bharat Dynamics rises; India expects to sell missiles to Philippines
data.description:
** Shares of Bharat Dynamics climb 3.4% to 1,175.50 rupees

** Stock set to snap five-session losing streak

** India expects to sell its short-range missiles to Philippines this year in a deal worth over $200 million, Reuters reports citing sources

** BARA, the manufacturer of the missiles, was one of the exhibitors at last year's Asian Defense and Security Exhibition in Manila

** Co and India's defence ministry did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment

** Philippine defence spokesperson declined to comment on deal's specifics or procurement plans but said the country's armed forces had "manifested it requires these capabilities"

data.title: DSM-Firmenich to Launch EUR1 Billion Share Repurchase Program
data.description:
DSM-Firmenich said Thursday it plans to launch a share buyback program of up to 1 billion euros to decrease its issued capital.

The chemicals company will start buying back up to 500 million euros of its shares in the second quarter before upsizing it to 1 billion euros after it closes its Feed Enzymes Alliance stake purchase.

DSM-Firmenich will repurchase shares until the second quarter of 2026.

data.title: Turkish 2024 current account deficit falls to below $10 bln
data.description:
Turkey's current account deficit narrowed to $9.97 billion in 2024 from nearly $40 billion a year earlier, the central bank said on Thursday, with interest rate hikes and gold trade restrictions having driven the decline.

According to economist Haluk Burumcekci's calculations, the current account deficit-to-GDP ratio fell to around 0.7% in 2024 from 3.6% the previous year. Turkey's long-term average current account deficit is around 3%.

The government's medium-term programme forecast a current account deficit-to-GDP ratio of 1.7%.

A Reuters poll forecast that the current account deficit will rise to $18 billion in 2025.

In December, the current account deficit widened to $4.65 billion, exceeding the Reuters poll forecast of $4 billion and rising from a deficit of $2.73 billion in November.

Excluding gold and energy, the current account showed a net surplus of $2.47 billion in December, while the goods deficit was $6.24 million.

In 2024, the current account deficit was mainly financed through direct investments, with a net inflow of $4.7 billion, portfolio investments with a net inflow of $11.7 billion and loans with a net inflow of $26.2 billion.

The currency and deposits item, along with trade credits, had a negative impact on the deficit, amounting to $14.0 billion and $5.4 billion respectively.

While net gold and energy imports halved in 2024 to $13.6 billion, net energy imports produced a deficit of $49.1 billion, $3.6 billion less than the previous year.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hong Kong Government Mulling to Move to WTO over Additional US Tariffs
data.description:
The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government will file a complaint with the World Trade Organization (WTO) against the likely additional 10% tariffs imposed by the US government on Hong Kong products, Hong Kong Business reported Tuesday.

The Hong Kong SAR said will launch procedures in line with the WTO Dispute Settlement Mechanism against the US' unreasonable measures to defend its legitimate rights. The Hong Kong SAR Government urged the US to take urgent action to rectify the notice and remove the suspension of accepting inbound postal items containing goods from Hong Kong Post on a priority basis, The publication said, citing a government statement.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Intuitive Surgical Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,517,854, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Gary Loeb, Executive Vice President & Chief Legal and Compliance Officer, on February 12, 2025, sold 2,600 shares in Intuitive Surgical for $1,517,854. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Loeb has control over a total of 3,176 common shares of the company, with 3,176 shares held directly.

SEC Filing:

data.title: Nissan, Honda and Mitsubishi Motors terminate MOU regarding consideration of tripartite collaboration
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 13, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Nissan Motor Co., Ltd. ("Nissan"), Honda Motor Co., Ltd. ("Honda"), and Mitsubishi Motors Corporation ("Mitsubishi Motors") today agreed to terminate their MOU regarding the consideration of the structure for a tripartite collaboration, in light of the termination of the MOU signed on December 23 last year regarding the consideration of a business integration between Nissan and Honda.

Going forward, the three companies will collaborate within the framework of a strategic partnership aimed at the era of intelligence and electrified vehicles. This framework was established with the MOU signed on August 1 last year, striving to create new value and maximize the corporate value of each company.

Source: Mitsubishi MotorsHonda Motor Co, LtdNissan

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Swiss annual inflation eases to lowest rate in nearly four years
data.description:
Swiss annual inflation slowed to 0.4% in January, its lowest rate since April 2021 and in line with expectations, official data showed on Thursday, creating more scope for interest rate cuts by the Swiss National Bank.

In December, Swiss annual inflation stood at 0.6%. Compared with the previous month, prices fell in January by 0.1%, the data from the Federal Statistics Office showed, also in line with consensus forecast of a Reuters poll.

The SNB, which targets an inflation rate of between 0 and 2%, has already said it could move its benchmark lending rate into negative territory if necessary.

It slashed the rate by 50 basis points to 0.5% at its December meeting, and markets expect it to lower borrowing costs further when it makes its next decision in March.

Thomas Gitzel, chief economist at VP Bank Group, said the issue was not whether the SNB will cut its key interest rate to zero, but whether it will do it as soon as next month.

"The really big question is what monetary policy will look like once the zero mark is reached," he said in a note, noting that the SNB could pursue negative interest rates and also had the option of intervening in the foreign exchange market.

"However, once interest rates reach zero, the SNB could also pause for a longer period and observe inflationary developments. The latter is probably the most likely scenario."

data.title: Heard on the Street Wednesday Recap: Egg Faced — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

Inflation came in hotter-than-expected. The consumer-price index rose 3% from a year earlier in January, up from a 2.9% pace in December. Core consumer prices, which exclude food and energy, were up 3.3% from a year earlier, compared with economist expectations for a 3.1% rise. Egg prices hit a new record high.

Stocks took the inflation news surprisingly well. The Dow Jones Industrial Average opened the session down almost 500 points, but by the end of the day investors had calmed somewhat. The Dow closed down 225 points, or 0.5%, while the S&P 500 fell 0.3%. The Nasdaq finished flat.

Bonds were another story. Treasury prices fell on the inflation readings, sending yields sharply higher. The 10-year note yield rose 0.10 percentage point to 4.636%, its biggest one-day gain since Dec. 18.

The Fed might stand pat this year. Investors increasingly think it is possible that the Federal Reserve is already done cutting rates. After the CPI print, futures markets are pricing in a 29.6% chance that the Fed's target rate will be unchanged from now at its meeting in December, up from a 19.6% chance one day ago, according to CME FedWatch.

CVS Health stock rallied. The pharmacy chain and insurer rose 15%, making the stock the top gainer in the S&P 500 on better-than-expected earnings and guidance. The company's new chief executive said there has been significant progress in stabilizing its insurance unit Aetna which, like other insurers, has lately struggled with higher-than-expected medical costs.

  • Check out all of the day's live narkets updates here.
  • Listen to WSJ Minute Briefing's audio summary of the day in markets.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Finland's Neste Flags 600 Job Cuts in Cost-cutting Move
data.description:
Neste on Thursday said it expects to lay off 600 employees globally as it moves to streamline its operating model to boost profitability and reduce external costs.

Consequently, the renewable energy group commenced change negotiations for the planned job cuts, of which 450 are in Finland. The positions set to be affected are within Neste's oil products and renewable products business segments.

The company aims to deliver annual cost savings of 65 million euros from the performance improvement program.

data.title: Concurrent Technologies to Begin Trading in US
data.description:
Concurrent Technologies will start trading on the OTCQX Best Market in the US on Thursday under the ticker symbol 'COTGF,' it said the same day.

The computer products maker's shares will continue trading on the London stock exchange's AIM segment. The company will not issue new shares as part of the move.

data.title: Genmab rises on better-than-expected Q4, guidance
data.description:
** Shares in Genmab rise 4.6% after the Danish drugmaker reported its Q4 operating income and guidance above expectations

** J.P.Morgan highlights EBIT and EBIT guidance beats as "small positives", the beats coming as a result of lower R&D spending

** JPM also points to Q4 revenues 1% ahead of company consensus which was driven by a higher-than-expected cancer drug Darzalex royalty rate of 19%

** Van Lanschot Kempen adds that the continued Darzalex sales growth can provide a "robust" royalty stream for the coming years

** Both JPM and Kempen note that the HexaBody-CD38 programme weighs on the stock price and see Johnson & Johnson's decision in Q1 2025 as potentially unburdening the shares

** The shares are on track for their best day in 2 months, if the gains hold

data.title: Al Wathba National Insurance Resumes Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
Al Wathba National Insurance (ADX:AWNIC) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Thursday, according to a same-day release.

The trading suspension was lifted after the Abu Dhabi-based insurer published its full-year 2024 financial results.

data.title: MGM China's Market-Share Gains, Dividend Payout Should Support Near-Term Stability — Market Talk
data.description: MGM China Holdings' market-share gains and potential dividend payout could support near-term stability for the stock, according to Citi analysts in a research note. The Macau casino operator's 4Q net revenue grew about 3%, and the company reported record-high Ebitda for 2024, the analysts note. MGM's market share increased 0.9 percentage point on quarter to about 15.6% in 4Q. Citi expects MGM China's board to declare a final dividend in mid-March and maintain its around 50% payout ratio. The implied dividend yield of about 6% would provide investors with downside risk protection in the near term, the analysts say. Citi keeps a buy rating with a target price of HK$13.25. Shares closed 6.5% higher at HK$10.34. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: European Stocks Rise on Ukraine Ceasefire Hopes — Market Talk
data.description: European stocks open higher amid expectations of a truce in Ukraine after President Trump said he is ready to negotiate with Russia. Although it is unclear whether a truce in the conflict would lead to a removal of sanctions on Russia and improved energy supply for Europe, investors could see it as a logical next step, Barclays analysts say in a research note. This could lift sentiment on the energy intensive sectors and manufacturing plays, the bank says. DAX, Germany's index of blue chips, jumps 1% in early trade with Siemens, BASF and auto makers Mercedes-Benz, Volkswagen and BMW among the biggest gainers. The Euro Stoxx 50 is up 0.90%, while France's CAC 40 climbs 0.87% and Switzerland's leading index rises 0.79%. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

data.title: TKO Group Holdings Insider Bought Shares Worth $68,128,471, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Patrick Whitesell, 10% Owner, on February 10, 2025, executed a purchase for 390,471 shares in TKO Group Holdings for $68,128,471. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Whitesell has control over a total of 4,404,467 Class A common shares of the company, with 4,404,467 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

data.title: Great Portland Estates Secures GBP1.6 Million in Annual Rent at London Property
data.description:
Great Portland Estates leased 6,700 square feet of refurbished fully managed office space at its Piccadilly Estate in London, securing 1.6 million pounds sterling in annual rent.

The four tenants are family offices or from the financial services sector, occupying their new space by the end of February, according to a Thursday filing by the real estate company.

The new leases bring the total flex space at the 201,000-square-foot property to 29,000 square feet, representing 60% of the let office space.

data.title: India's BCL Industries falls after Q3 profit drop
data.description:
** Shares of BCL Industries fall 4.9% to 41.30 rupees

** Co's Q3 consol net profit fell 37.3% y/y to 192 mln rupees ($2.2 mln)

** Q3 revenue rose 18.2% y/y, while expenses jumped 20.7%

** Stock set to fall for sixth straight session

** BCLN fell 24% 2024

($1 = 86.8925 Indian rupees)

data.title: Delivery Hero to Buyback EUR1 Billion of 2025, 2026, 2027 Bonds
data.description:
Delivery Hero intends to repurchase 1 billion euros of outstanding convertible bonds due 2025, 2026, and 2027, according to a Thursday release.

The German food delivery company offered to buy back all of the 2025 and 2026 bonds and up to 350 million euros of the 2027 bonds. The outstanding bonds include 48.1 million euros of 2025 bonds, 593.5 million euros of 2026 bonds, and 875 million euros of 2027 bonds.

The buybacks, which are expected to optimize the company's capital structure, will be financed through the proceeds from the initial public offering of its unit Talabat (DFM:TALABAT) on the Dubai Financial Market.

data.title: Kenyan shilling edges up against dollar
data.description:
The Kenyan shilling strengthened a little against the U.S. dollar on Thursday, bolstered by foreign exchange inflows from the hospitality sector, traders said.

At 0838 GMT the shilling was quoted at 128.90/129.40 per dollar, compared to 129.00/129.50 at the close of Wednesday's session.

data.title: COMMENT-Turkey's currency is clearly broken
data.description:
Turkey's currency is clearly broken, with the lira falling regardless of monetary policy or investor sentiment.

While few were surprised that the lira suffered during a period of highly unusual monetary policy, it is more surprising that it has continued to fall since more conventional policy has been applied.

Turkey's currency continued to drop when the nation's interest rate reached a stratospheric 50%, and is extending the slide now rates are being slashed.

The favourable opinion of ratings agencies has made not a jot of difference, and unless well hedged, those investing in Turkish assets will be suffering.

Anyone holding the lira is hurting with the currency carving out record lows on an almost daily basis, with little relief in sight even if the dollar slides in wider markets.

The lira is crashing versus the euro, which is the currency of Turkey's biggest export market, and the pound.

This week EUR/TRY reached 37.67, its highest point since early November, and is perilously close to the 38.43 all-time high reached last August. The lira has traded 45.20 versus Britain's pound, within touching distance of last September's record peak of 45.88.

Since March 2024, while the interest rate has been amazingly high, USD/TRY has risen almost 14%, reaching 36.11 this month. The pace of the rally has accelerated since Donald Trump won the U.S. election.

USD/TRY was trading around 30 when interest rates approached their peak in January 2024 and could reach 40 before this year ends.

For more click on

data.title: HBX Group Shares Slip on Spanish Stock Market Debut
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Shares in Spanish travel technology HBX Group fell slightly on the company's first day of trading on the Madrid stock exchange.

The stock is currently trading around 11.30 euros compared with its initial public offering price of 11.50 euros announced on Feb. 10, implying a market capitalization of 2.79 billion euros ($2.90 billion).

The group--which houses Hotelbeds, Bedsonline and Roiback--has raised 725 million euros of new money by issuing 63.04 million shares as part of its IPO.

It plans to use the money to settle the deferred sale consideration due in respect of the selldown of shares of different members of the group.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com
